caption:
Why Japanese tsuriami knitting produces sweatshirts that modern machines can’t replicate.

#amekaji #menswear #ivystyle #heritagemenswear #madeinjapan
transcript: The best sweatshirts in the world are probably from Japan and it's not just the cotton, it's how the fabric is made. The machines that make them, known as Tsuriyami knitting machines, were originally imported into Japan from Europe in the early 20th century to mechanized textile production. In Japan, a handful of workshops, mostly in the Wakayama prefecture, kept using these machines long after the rest of the world had switched to high-speed knitting. They knit the fabric very slowly, one meter at a time, using only one weight of the yarn. Because of that slow process, the cotton isn't stretched or stressed, creating a knit that's soft, durable and remarkably resilient. The fabric is produced in a continuous tube with no side seams, giving it a natural drape and comfort that modern machines just can't replicate. The end result is a sweatshirt that feels alive on your body, built to last decades rather than years, and that's why I think Japanese Tsuriyami sweatshirts are among the best in the world.
